name: Joshua Palmer
headline: Buffalo's Field-Stretcher Hunts a Bigger 2026 Slice
body:
Palmer's first Buffalo season was a quiet one on paper, but the underlying work hints at upside. Across 12 games he ran routes on just 39.7% of dropbacks, drew a 7.7% target share, and turned 37 looks into 22 grabs for 303 yards. The downfield role survived the move: 11.46 air yards per target and a 19.3 air-yards-per-reception mark rank among the deepest on the roster, and he posted a healthy 0.182 EPA per play with a 43.2% success rate despite zero touchdowns.

The story is opportunity, not ability. His 2024 baseline in Los Angeles, a 13.2% target share, 0.388 EPA per play, and 52.3% success rate, shows what fuller usage unlocks. With six red-zone targets already and consistent vertical separation, even a modest snap bump pushes him into flex relevance. He is a cheap late-round dart with real spike-week equity in a high-volume passing attack.
